title: Fish pie
subtitle: Cod, smoked haddock, and prawns under a buttery mash
excerpt: A mix of white fish, smoked haddock, and prawns in a creamy sauce, topped with mashed potato and baked until golden. The sauce is made from the poaching milk so nothing is wasted. A classic British comfort dish that works as a family supper or a dinner party centrepiece.
sourceNotes: Fish pie is one of the definitive British comfort dishes. The combination of white fish, smoked fish, and shellfish in a creamy sauce under mashed potato has been standard since at least the Victorian era. The French gratin de fruits de mer uses a similar logic but with different fish and often breadcrumbs rather than mash.

content.text: Poach the fish in milk before making the sauce, then use that same milk to build the béchamel. The milk absorbs the flavour of the fish and transforms the sauce. Do not discard the poaching milk. It is the most flavourful liquid in the kitchen at that point.
Hard-boiled eggs in fish pie are traditional and optional. They add substance and textural contrast. Cut them into quarters and press into the fish mixture before adding the mash.
Poach the fish and make the sauce

Put the {{cod-fillet}} and {{smoked-haddock}} in a wide pan. Pour over the {{whole-milk}} for the sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer and poach for 5 minutes, until just opaque. Lift the fish out with a slotted spoon into a large baking dish, breaking into large flakes. Reserve all the poaching milk. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a saucepan, add the {{plain-flour}}, and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the warm poaching milk until smooth. Stir over medium heat until the sauce thickens. Season with {{dijon-mustard}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Add the {{prawns-cooked}} to the fish in the dish. Pour the sauce over.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Boil the {{potato}} until completely tender. Drain and steam-dry. Mash with the {{unsalted-butter}} and warm {{whole-milk}} for the topping until smooth. Season. Spoon the mash over the fish and sauce in the baking dish. Spread evenly and rough up the surface with a fork. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the top is golden and the sauce is bubbling at the edges.

items.fix: Poach until just opaque, 5 minutes for most fillet pieces. Overcooked fish releases liquid that thins the sauce
items.cause: Fish overcooked during poaching, releasing water
items.symptom: Watery sauce pooling at the edges

items.fix: Ensure the mash is stiff enough to spread without sinking. Cool the fish filling slightly before adding the mash
items.cause: Mash too soft or filling too thin
items.symptom: Mash topping sinking into the filling

items.fix: Roughing up the surface with a fork creates peaks that catch the heat and brown. If still pale after 30 minutes, switch on the grill for 3 minutes
items.cause: Oven temperature too low, or surface not roughed up

content.text: Fish pie is a Friday supper dish in Catholic households, a dinner party centrepiece when made with good fish, and a family comfort food year-round. It is one of the few complete dishes in British cooking: protein, sauce, and starchy topping in one.
